article_meta.title: Why the US needs China's rare earths16 October 2025ShareSaveAyeshea PereraAsia Digital Editor, Singapore ShareSaveGetty

reasoning: One valid author-prose bias was flagged, but there was an attribution-rule violation and multiple clear author-level bias instances were missed. The model also included a questionable Bias by Omission claim.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Item 2 cites and relies on quoted, attributed language ('economic coercion', 'power grab') as part of the flagged bias, violating the rule to avoid quoted/attributed source language.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Two of three items are not defensible: the Slant item leans on quoted/attributed US language and a debatable imbalance claim; the Bias by Omission claim about Greenland speculates missing context without clear necessity.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed several clear author-prose spins/loaded terms: 'chokehold', 'tightened its grip', 'hit the US hard', and 'stand to be severely impacted.'
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: For the one genuinely biased author-prose item ('major blow'), the label Opinion Statements Presented as Fact is defensible.
